headlines.title: Chris Mason: Dissent fizzes again at the top of the Labour Party
headlines.description: The resignations of the defence secretary and armed forces minister pile the pressure further on the PM's leadership.

headlines.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX raises $75bn ahead of record stock market debut
headlines.description: The public sale is also expected to make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire.

headlines.title: Ministers want 60% of children walking or cycling to school by 2035
headlines.description: Thousands of safer paths and crossings are promised to help people become more active in England's towns and cities.

headlines.title: Grooming survivors left with criminal records still being failed, BBC told
headlines.description: Victims who were abused and prosecuted as children are still being failed, says grooming report author.

headlines.title: Nineteen now arrested over Northern Ireland disorder with house targeted in arson
headlines.description: Thursday night was relatively quiet although police say an arson attack in north Belfast is linked to the disorder.

headlines.title: 'I spent uni savings on getting my teeth fixed' - the real cost of NHS dentist shortage
headlines.description: People tell BBC Your Voice the rising cost of private dentistry is putting them in a difficult position.

headlines.title: Taylor Swift makes tearful 21-minute speech as she joins Songwriters Hall of Fame
headlines.description: The singer thanks her family as she becomes the youngest woman to be inducted into the Hall of Fame.

headlines.title: UK economy contracts as Iran war impact felt
headlines.description: The economy shrank slightly in April as the Iran war began to have an impact on businesses, official data shows.

headlines.title: My friends always want to split the bill equally, how do I say no?
headlines.description: It is never easy to speak up when a fellow diner says "let's just divide it!"

headlines.title: 'No-one knows it's on' - NBA Finals feed US World Cup apathy
headlines.description: With the World Cup under way, how excited are people across the United States - and do they even know it is on?

headlines.title: From near death to tournament hero - Jimenez's tears for Mexico
headlines.description: From nearly dying on the pitch to scoring for Mexico at the World Cup - Raul Jimenez has completed a remarkable comeback.

headlines.title: The World Cup of adverts: How brands are competing to entertain, not simply sell
headlines.description: Kim Kardashian, Timothée Chalamet and Susan Boyle feature in ambitious ads ahead of the World Cup.

headlines.title: Tartan Army feels the heat in Boston as World Cup kicks off
headlines.description: Visitors are facing 31C temperatures in the city, with the soaring heat expected to continue into Saturday when Scotland's men face Haiti.

headlines.title: David Hockney: Art's great innovator whose vivid paintings made him a household name
headlines.description: The proud Yorkshireman, who also painted iconic pictures of America, was routinely described as Britain's greatest artist.

headlines.title: 'I've never been this good' – revolutionary immune reset puts lupus in remission
headlines.description: Patients on the trial have not needed medication to manage their condition.

headlines.title: Why the economics make this the craziest World Cup ever
headlines.description: From trade wars to soaring ticket prices, the 2026 World Cup is unlike any before it. Faisal Islam explores what this tournament reveals about our changing global economy.

headlines.title: The unknown man in my mother's coffin: A year after Air India crash, families still waiting for answers
headlines.description: Relatives urge Indian authorities for answers after receiving the misidentified, and in one case mixed, remains of their loved ones.

headlines.title: 'They destroyed the future': Palestinian anger at rise in Israeli demolitions in East Jerusalem
headlines.description: Palestinian homes are being pulled down in occupied East Jerusalem to make way for a park.

headlines.title: Weekly quiz: Which Sesame Street character got in trouble with New Yorkers?
headlines.description: How much attention did you pay to what happened in the world over the past seven days?

headlines.title: Watch: Three things to know about SpaceX's stock market debut
headlines.description: The BBC's Samira Hussain explains everything you need to know about SpaceX's historic IPO.

headlines.title: South Africa trolled by African fans in wake of World Cup loss
headlines.description: The taunting reflects anger from parts of the continent over reports of xenophobia in South Africa.

headlines.title: Summer warmth to return for weekend but heatwave chances fade
headlines.description: After a wet start to June it will be drier and warmer for the weekend, as Ben Rich explains.

headlines.title: UK signals it may block payout to British Steel owner
headlines.description: Chinese firm Jingye is seeking compensation after the government decided to nationalise the company.

headlines.title: Meningitis B vaccine to be offered to a million young people after outbreaks
headlines.description: The decision for the one-off vaccine programme follows the unprecedented outbreak in Kent this year.

headlines.title: Thai Princess Bajrakitiyabha dies after more than three years in coma
headlines.description: Princess Bajrakitiyabha, the king's eldest daughter, collapsed in December 2022 while exercising her dogs.

headlines.title: Ex-UK political aide Steve Hilton would overhaul California as governor, he tells BBC
headlines.description: The Trump-backed candidate is running as a Republican in the deeply liberal state on a platform that he says isn't beholden to party ideology.

headlines.title: Our sons are playing for two different countries at the World Cup
headlines.description: Jack and Heather Souttar are heading to the US to support their sons, who play for Scotland and Australia.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Why Has Defence Secretary John Healey Resigned?
headlines.description: Defence Secretary John Healey resigns in latest blow to Starmer.

headlines.title: The Democrats fight back Trump-style in Maine… but have they messed up?
headlines.description: New scandals and allegations emerge against Democrat Graham Platner in Maine

headlines.title: Williamson ends New Zealand career in middle of series with England
headlines.description: Former New Zealand captain Kane Williamson - his country's all-time leading run-scorer - retires from all international cricket with immediate effect.

headlines.title: Gasly's Monaco third place reinstated after appeal
headlines.description: Pierre Gasly is reinstated into third place in the Monaco Grand Prix, after the stewards rescind the two penalties he received for pit-lane speeding.

headlines.title: Watford great Jackett dies aged 64
headlines.description: Watford legend and former Wales international Kenny Jackett dies at the age of 64.

headlines.title: A new way to experience the World Cup - only on BBC Sport
headlines.description: Ever wanted to see a World Cup match from every angle? How about a full-pitch tactical view? Now you can on the BBC.
